<findings>

[observation] [warning] High coupling (0.6) — 30 imports target "prd-tree-operations"
[observation] [warning] Coupling of 0.6 is at the warning threshold; this zone imports from three separate domain zones (unit-core, unit-store, unit-analyze), making it vulnerable to breakage from changes in any of them.
[observation] [info] Grouping shared CLI utilities (errors.ts, output.ts, validate-input.ts) in src/cli/ rather than scattering them across command files is a clean pattern that should be preserved.
[observation] [warning] Reverse imports from unit-core and unit-store back into the cli zone violate the expected unidirectional dependency from CLI → domain; investigate whether domain modules have leaked CLI concerns.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "cli-command-layer" has files across 6 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [warning] chunked-review-state.ts is an ~870-line pure state machine explicitly documented as having 'no I/O' — move it out of src/cli/commands/ to src/analyze/ or src/review/ to match its domain role. Its current location inflates cli-command-layer's file count and hides a substantial domain concern inside the CLI layer.
[suggestion] [warning] cli-command-layer is the only zone with BOTH high coupling (0.6) AND low cohesion (0.4) — the combination makes it the most fragile production zone in the codebase. Changes to any of three upstream zones (unit-core, unit-store, unit-analyze) can break it, while its low internal cohesion ensures failures propagate unpredictably. Splitting command files from shared CLI utilities (output.ts, errors.ts, validate-input.ts, token-format.ts) into separate zones would reduce both metrics.
[suggestion] [info] token-format.ts should be split into two files: a pure formatting module (formatAggregateTokenUsage, formatBudgetWarnings) and a budget-check orchestration module (preflightBudgetCheck). Mixing IO-bound store access with presentation-only string formatting in a file named 'token-format' violates single responsibility and makes the file's scope invisible from its name.
[pattern] [warning] The CLI zone functions as an unintended shared vocabulary layer: 30 reverse imports from unit-core (10), unit-store (7), and unit-analyze (13) all reach upward into CLI for utilities and types. This pattern indicates CLI-layer symbols have been promoted to cross-domain status without deliberate design.
[relationship] [critical] CLIError defined in src/cli/errors.ts is imported by src/core/move.ts, making a domain module depend on a CLI module. This inverts the expected flow and should be resolved by moving CLIError to a shared errors or foundation layer.

</findings>

<insights>

- High coupling (0.6) — 30 imports target "prd-tree-operations"
- Coupling at 0.6 hits the warning threshold — this zone imports from unit-core (30), unit-store (26), and unit-analyze (13), making it the most connected zone in the graph and a hotspot for ripple-effect changes.
- cli receives imports from unit-core (10) and unit-store (7), meaning the dependency flows both directions; CLI modules should only be consumers, not providers, of domain logic.
- token-format.ts and output.ts as entry points suggest formatting concerns are reused across commands — confirm these don't contain any business logic that belongs in the domain layer.
- Coupling of 0.6 is at the warning threshold; this zone imports from three separate domain zones (unit-core, unit-store, unit-analyze), making it vulnerable to breakage from changes in any of them.
- Reverse imports from unit-core and unit-store back into the cli zone violate the expected unidirectional dependency from CLI → domain; investigate whether domain modules have leaked CLI concerns.
- Grouping shared CLI utilities (errors.ts, output.ts, validate-input.ts) in src/cli/ rather than scattering them across command files is a clean pattern that should be preserved.
- Zone "cli-command-layer" has files across 6 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- src/core/move.ts imports CLIError from ../cli/errors.js — a domain module directly depending on a CLI type, confirming that CLI error types have leaked into domain logic rather than being defined in a shared foundation layer
- CLIError defined in src/cli/errors.ts is imported by src/core/move.ts, making a domain module depend on a CLI module. This inverts the expected flow and should be resolved by moving CLIError to a shared errors or foundation layer.
- The CLI zone functions as an unintended shared vocabulary layer: 30 reverse imports from unit-core (10), unit-store (7), and unit-analyze (13) all reach upward into CLI for utilities and types. This pattern indicates CLI-layer symbols have been promoted to cross-domain status without deliberate design.
- cli-command-layer is the only production zone combining high coupling (0.6) and low cohesion (0.4) simultaneously — this combination is the canonical definition of a fragile zone: many inbound dependencies can break it, and its low internal cohesion means changes cascade unpredictably within it
- chunked-review-state.ts (~870 lines) lives in src/cli/commands/ but its own header comment reads 'Pure logic (no I/O, fully testable)' — a domain-level interactive-review state machine with no CLI I/O is misplaced in the CLI commands directory; it belongs in src/analyze/ or a dedicated src/review/ module
- token-format.ts mixes two unrelated concerns under one file name: pure formatting functions (formatAggregateTokenUsage, formatBudgetWarnings) and an IO-bound data-loading orchestration function (preflightBudgetCheck that resolves a store, reads config, and reads logs). The 'format' name only describes half the file's responsibility.
- cli-command-layer is the only zone with BOTH high coupling (0.6) AND low cohesion (0.4) — the combination makes it the most fragile production zone in the codebase. Changes to any of three upstream zones (unit-core, unit-store, unit-analyze) can break it, while its low internal cohesion ensures failures propagate unpredictably. Splitting command files from shared CLI utilities (output.ts, errors.ts, validate-input.ts, token-format.ts) into separate zones would reduce both metrics.
- chunked-review-state.ts is an ~870-line pure state machine explicitly documented as having 'no I/O' — move it out of src/cli/commands/ to src/analyze/ or src/review/ to match its domain role. Its current location inflates cli-command-layer's file count and hides a substantial domain concern inside the CLI layer.
- token-format.ts should be split into two files: a pure formatting module (formatAggregateTokenUsage, formatBudgetWarnings) and a budget-check orchestration module (preflightBudgetCheck). Mixing IO-bound store access with presentation-only string formatting in a file named 'token-format' violates single responsibility and makes the file's scope invisible from its name.
- [call graph] 835 internal calls, 90 outgoing, 400 incoming (cohesion: 0.9, coupling: 0.1)

</insights>
